briefdescription.para: These constants specify the options that may be set or retrieved with the driver, each is a unique bit mask such that multiple options may be specified.

detaileddescription.para: These constants indicate the available options in active state.Starts break transmission

briefdescription.para: Stops break transmission.
Reset the receive timeout.
Reset the transmitter.
Reset the receiver.
Assert the RTS bit.
Assert the DTR bit.
Turn on flow control mode.

description.para: The UART can operate in one of four modes: Normal, Local Loopback, Remote Loopback, or automatic echo.

briefdescription.para: Normal Mode.
Auto Echo Mode.
Local Loopback Mode.
Remote Loopback Mode.

description.para: These constants specify the data format that the driver supports.
The data format includes the number of data bits, the number of stop bits and parity.

briefdescription.para: 8 data bits
7 data bits
6 data bits
No parity.
Mark parity.
parity
Odd parity.
Even parity.
2 stop bits
1.5 stop bits
1 stop bit

description.para: These constants specify the handler events that an application can handle using its specific handler function.
Note that these constants are not bit mask, so only one event can be passed to an application at a time.

briefdescription.para: Data receiving done.
A receive timeout occurred.
Data transmission done.
A receive error detected.
Modem status changed.
A receive parity, frame, break error detected.
A receive overrun error detected.
Wait for 1 sec in worst case.
Get the UART Channel Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.

briefdescription.para: Get the UART Mode Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register.

briefdescription.para: Set the UART Mode Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
is the value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XUartPs_SetModeControl(XUartPs *InstancePtr, u16 RegisterValue)

briefdescription.para: Enable the transmitter and receiver of the UART.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

briefdescription.para: Disable the transmitter and receiver of the UART.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

briefdescription.para: Determine if the transmitter FIFO is empty.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if a byte can be sent
FALSE if the Transmitter Fifo is not empty

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* XUartPs_Handler)(void *CallBackRef, u32 Event, u32 EventData)
memberdef.name: XUartPs_Handler

briefdescription.para: This data type defines a handler that an application defines to communicate with interrupt system to retrieve state information about an application.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the handler, and is passed back to the upper layer when the handler is called. It is used to find the device driver instance.
contains one of the event constants indicating events that have occurred.
contains the number of bytes sent or received at the time of the call for send and receive events and contains the modem status for modem events.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XUartPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xuartps.h for the definition of XUartPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XUartPs instance such that it is ready to be used.

detaileddescription.para: The data format of the device is setup for 8 data bits, 1 stop bit, and no parity by default. The baud rate is set to a default value specified by Config->DefaultBaudRate if set, otherwise it is set to 19.2K baud. The receive FIFO threshold is set for 8 bytes. The default operating mode of the driver is polled mode.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
Config
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XUartPs driver.
EffectiveAddr
is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, pass in the physical address instead.
- XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful - XST_UART_BAUD_ERROR if the baud rate is not possible because the inputclock frequency is not divisible with an acceptable amount of error
The default configuration for the UART after initialization is:

listitem.para: FIFO's are enabled with a receive threshold of 8 bytes
The RX timeout is enabled with a timeout of 1 (4 char times)

briefdescription.para: This functions sends the specified buffer using the device in either polled or interrupt driven mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function is non-blocking, if the device is busy sending data, it will return and indicate zero bytes were sent. Otherwise, it fills the TX FIFO as much as it can, and return the number of bytes sent.
In a polled mode, this function will only send as much data as TX FIFO can buffer. The application may need to call it repeatedly to send the entire buffer.

In interrupt mode, this function will start sending the specified buffer, then the interrupt handler will continue sending data until the entire buffer has been sent. A callback function, as specified by the application, will be called to indicate the completion of sending.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
BufferPtr
is pointer to a buffer of data to be sent.
NumBytes
contains the number of bytes to be sent. A value of zero will stop a previous send operation that is in progress in interrupt mode. Any data that was already put into the transmit FIFO will be sent.
The number of bytes actually sent.
The number of bytes is not asserted so that this function may be called with a value of zero to stop an operation that is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: This function attempts to receive a specified number of bytes of data from the device and store it into the specified buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function works for both polled or interrupt driven modes. It is non-blocking.

In a polled mode, this function will only receive the data already in the RX FIFO. The application may need to call it repeatedly to receive the entire buffer. Polled mode is the default mode of operation for the device.

In interrupt mode, this function will start the receiving, if not the entire buffer has been received, the interrupt handler will continue receiving data until the entire buffer has been received. A callback function, as specified by the application, will be called to indicate the completion of the receiving or error conditions.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance
BufferPtr
is pointer to buffer for data to be received into
NumBytes
is the number of bytes to be received. A value of zero will stop a previous receive operation that is in progress in interrupt mode.
The number of bytes received.
The number of bytes is not asserted so that this function may be called with a value of zero to stop an operation that is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: Sets the baud rate for the device.

detaileddescription.para: Checks the input value for validity and also verifies that the requested rate can be configured to within the maximum error range specified by XUARTPS_MAX_BAUD_ERROR_RATE. If the provided rate is not possible, the current setting is unchanged.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance
to be set

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if everything configured as expected
XST_UART_BAUD_ERROR if the requested rate is not available because there was too much error

briefdescription.para: Sets the options for the specified driver instance.

detaileddescription.para: The options are implemented as bit masks such that multiple options may be enabled or disabled simultaneously.

The GetOptions function may be called to retrieve the currently enabled options. The result is ORed in the desired new settings to be enabled and ANDed with the inverse to clear the settings to be disabled. The resulting value is then used as the options for the SetOption function call.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
contains the options to be set which are bit masks contained in the file xuartps.h and named XUARTPS_OPTION_*.

briefdescription.para: Gets the options for the specified driver instance.

detaileddescription.para: The options are implemented as bit masks such that multiple options may be enabled or disabled simultaneously.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
The current options for the UART. The options are bit masks that are contained in the file xuartps.h and named XUARTPS_OPTION_*.

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the receive FIFO trigger level.

detaileddescription.para: The receive trigger level specifies the number of bytes in the receive FIFO that cause a receive data event (interrupt) to be generated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
contains the trigger level to set.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the receive FIFO trigger level.

detaileddescription.para: The receive trigger level indicates the number of bytes in the receive FIFO that cause a receive data event (interrupt) to be generated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

simplesect.para: The current receive FIFO trigger level. This is a value from 0-31.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the modem status from the specified UART.

detaileddescription.para: The modem status indicates any changes of the modem signals. This function allows the modem status to be read in a polled mode. The modem status is updated whenever it is read such that reading it twice may not yield the same results.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
The modem status which are bit masks that are contained in the file xuartps.h and named XUARTPS_MODEM_*.
The bit masks used for the modem status are the exact bits of the modem status register with no abstraction.

briefdescription.para: This function determines if the specified UART is sending data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the UART is sending data
FALSE if UART is not sending data

briefdescription.para: This function gets the operational mode of the UART.

detaileddescription.para: The UART can operate in one of four modes: Normal, Local Loopback, Remote Loopback, or automatic echo.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
The operational mode is specified by constants defined in xuartps.h. The constants are named XUARTPS_OPER_MODE_*

briefdescription.para: This function sets the operational mode of the UART.

detaileddescription.para: The UART can operate in one of four modes: Normal, Local Loopback, Remote Loopback, or automatic echo.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
is the mode of the UART.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Flow Delay.

detaileddescription.para: 0 - 3: Flow delay inactive 4 - 32: If Flow Control mode is enabled, UART_rtsN is deactivated when the receive FIFO fills to this level.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
The Flow Delay is specified by constants defined in xuartps_hw.h. The constants are named XUARTPS_FLOWDEL*

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Flow Delay.

detaileddescription.para: 0 - 3: Flow delay inactive 4 - 63: If Flow Control mode is enabled, UART_rtsN is deactivated when the receive FIFO fills to this level.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
is the Setting for the flow delay.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Receive Timeout of the UART.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

simplesect.para: The current setting for receive time out.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Receive Timeout of the UART.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
setting allows the UART to detect an idle connection on the receiver data line. Timeout duration = RecvTimeout x 4 x Bit Period. 0 disables the timeout function.

briefdescription.para: Sets the data format for the device.

detaileddescription.para: The data format includes the baud rate, number of data bits, number of stop bits, and parity. It is the caller's responsibility to ensure that the UART is not sending or receiving data when this function is called.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
FormatPtr
is a pointer to a format structure containing the data format to be set.
XST_SUCCESS if the data format was successfully set.
XST_UART_BAUD_ERROR indicates the baud rate could not be set because of the amount of error with the baud rate and the input clock frequency.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if one of the parameters was not valid.
The data types in the format type, data bits and parity, are 32 bit fields to prevent a compiler warning. The asserts in this function will cause a warning if these fields are bytes.

briefdescription.para: Gets the data format for the specified UART.

detaileddescription.para: The data format includes the baud rate, number of data bits, number of stop bits, and parity.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.
is a pointer to a format structure that will contain the data format after this call completes.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance.

simplesect.para: The current interrupt mask. The mask indicates which interrupts are enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance
contains the interrupts to be enabled or disabled. A '1' enables an interrupt, and a '0' disables.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to an interrupt system by the application such that it can be called when an interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: contains a pointer to the driver instance

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler that will be called when an event (interrupt) occurs that needs application's attention.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XUartPs instance
FuncPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
None.
There is no assert on the CallBackRef since the driver doesn't know what it is (nor should it)

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the driver and hardware device.

detaileddescription.para: This self test performs a local loopback and verifies data can be sent and received.
The time for this test is proportional to the baud rate that has been set prior to calling this function.
The mode and control registers are restored before return.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XUartPs instance

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the test was successful
XST_UART_TEST_FAIL if the test failed looping back the data

detaileddescription.para: This function can hang if the hardware is not functioning properly.
